title: Replace 'Get Started' with a context-specific CTA
summary: AI-generated copy uses 'Get Started' as the universal CTA regardless of context, telling users nothing about what they are starting or what happens next.
instruction: Use CTAs that reflect the user's actual first action: 'Build your first dashboard', 'Create your workspace', 'Import your data' — not the generic 'Get Started'.
rationale: 'Get Started' does not tell users what they are starting or what will happen when they click. A context-specific CTA ('Build your first report') gives users actionable information about what the action entails.

violations: A landing page using 'Get Started' as the primary CTA with no product context.
Every section CTA on a page uses 'Get Started' for different actions (trial, demo, download).
controls: 'Build your first dashboard in under 2 minutes' as a CTA that sets expectations.
'Start your 14-day free trial (no credit card)' with explicit terms.
borderline: A well-known brand where 'Get Started' is part of a recognizable brand pattern and the user already understands the product.

exceptions.description: Brands where 'Get Started' is part of a consistent, tested design pattern with supporting context nearby. Internal tools where the audience already understands the action.
examples: Onboarding flow with 'Get Started' as the first of multi-step labels

detector.signalDescription: Find 'Get Started' button/link text. Check for nearby descriptive context that explains what the user is starting. Flag when 'Get Started' is the sole CTA descriptor.
limitations: An onboarding flow may use 'Get Started' as the first step label, which is appropriate.
Some A/B tested CTAs show 'Get Started' outperforming alternatives — these should be documented exceptions.

scenarios: Multi-step onboarding where 'Get Started' is the first step label — appropriate for flow context.
A/B test results showing 'Get Started' outperforms alternatives — documented preference should suppress.
A well-known consumer product where 'Get Started' is part of a consistent brand pattern with sufficient supporting context.
mitigations: Check for step-progression context (progress bar, step count). Accept documented A/B results as suppressible. Inspect enough nearby hero or section context to determine whether the action the CTA starts is described elsewhere in the view.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Replace 'Get Started' with a CTA that names the user's first action: 'Build your first [X]', 'Create your [Y]', 'Import your [Z]'.

fixturePlan.description: A landing page with 'Get Started' as the primary hero CTA and no product context.
Same page with 'Build your first workflow in 5 minutes' as the primary CTA.
An onboarding wizard with 'Get Started' as step 1 of a multi-step flow.
